overview: Orzhov Greasefang is a combo-focused Pioneer archetype that reanimates Parhelion II with Greasefang, Okiba Boss to create a fast two-turn clock. It synergizes with Monument to Endurance and multiple discard outlets to generate continuous card advantage and incremental damage. The deck is highly resilient, using The Mycosynth Gardens to stack triggers and maintaining a straightforward alternative game plan that doesn't interfere with its primary combo.
win_conditions: Reanimate Parhelion II with Greasefang, Okiba Boss, crew it, and attack to create two 4/4 Angel tokens for lethal damage.
Trigger Monument to Endurance repeatedly with discard outlets and The Mycosynth Gardens to generate card advantage, ramp, and incremental damage.
Use Overlord of the Balemurk to discard cards and reuse creatures each combat as a secondary win condition.

keep_criteria: Hands with Monument to Endurance and at least one discard outlet
Hands containing Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II
Hands with early interaction like Thoughtseize or Fatal Push
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking discard outlets to enable Monument to Endurance
Hands with only lands or high-cost permanents
Hands missing both combo pieces and interaction

examples.reason: Contains the core combo piece, a discard outlet to trigger Monument, a land base, and early interaction to protect the plan.

early_game: Play Monument to Endurance and discard Parhelion II to begin generating value.
Deploy cheap discard outlets like Seasoned Hallowblade, Guardian of New Benalia, or Fleeting Spirit to trigger Monument repeatedly.
Use Thoughtseize or Fatal Push to disrupt aggressive opponents or protect combo pieces.
mid_game: Reanimate Parhelion II using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and immediately crew it to attack.
Cast The Mycosynth Gardens to copy Monument to Endurance triggers and accelerate card advantage.
Use Sheltered by Ghosts on creatures that can gain indestructible via discard to protect against removal.
late_game: Stack Monument triggers with Seasoned Hallowblade and similar cards to create a snowball effect.
Use Takenuma, Abandoned Mire and Geier Reach Sanitarium to recur key creatures and draw cards.
Close out the game with Angel token attacks or incremental Monument damage.

key_cards.name: Greasefang, Okiba Boss
key_cards.notes: Returns Parhelion II from the graveyard with Haste to crew it and create Angel tokens.

key_cards.name: Parhelion II
key_cards.notes: Vehicle that creates two 4/4 Angel tokens when crewed, dealing 13 damage on turn one and eight more the next turn.

key_cards.name: Monument to Endurance
key_cards.notes: Triggers on discard to provide card advantage, ramp, and damage; synergizes with discard outlets.

key_cards.name: The Mycosynth Gardens
key_cards.notes: Functions as extra copies of Monument to Endurance to stack triggers for each discarded card.

key_cards.name: Seasoned Hallowblade
key_cards.notes: Cheap creature that enables multiple Monument triggers each turn and can crew Parhelion II if needed.

key_cards.name: Guardian of New Benalia
key_cards.notes: Provides repeated discard effects to generate value with Monument while having a decent body.

key_cards.name: Fleeting Spirit
key_cards.notes: Enables Monument triggers and can be recursed from the graveyard by exiling three cards.

key_cards.name: Overlord of the Balemurk
key_cards.notes: Puts cards directly into the graveyard and allows reusing any creature each combat.

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Protects the combo, removes key cards, provides information, and counters aggressive explosive turns.

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Best one-mana answer against small creatures like Mono Red Aggro, Angels, or Spirits.

key_cards.name: Sheltered by Ghosts
key_cards.notes: Slows down Aggro and protects permanents; safe to use on creatures that can gain indestructible via discard.

key_cards.name: Geier Reach Sanitarium
key_cards.notes: Recurring discard outlet that provides an extra draw or Bump in the Night with each Monument in play.

key_cards.name: Takenuma, Abandoned Mire
key_cards.notes: Feeds the graveyard and recurs creatures, primarily targeting Greasefang, Okiba Boss.

key_cards.name: Vanishing Verse
key_cards.notes: Handles Red Aggro, Rakdos Midrange, Izzet Phoenix, Selesnya Angels, and graveyard hate like Leyline of the Void and Rest in Peace.

key_cards.name: Unlicensed Hearse
key_cards.notes: Targeted graveyard hate that can be returned to play and then to hand with Greasefang; works against Izzet Phoenix, mirror, and Sacrifice.

sequencing_tips: Discard Parhelion II early to enable Monument to Endurance triggers before reanimating it.
Play Monument to Endurance before casting discard outlets to maximize triggers per turn.
Use The Mycosynth Gardens to copy Monument triggers after playing discard outlets to stack value.
Target Sheltered by Ghosts on creatures that can gain indestructible through discard effects to mitigate removal.
Crew Parhelion II immediately after reanimating it with Greasefang to attack on the same turn.

matchups.advice: Side in Vanishing Verse and Seam Rip. Use Fatal Push and Sheltered by Ghosts to slow down small creatures and protect your board.
Side in Vanishing Verse. Use Thoughtseize to disrupt their combo or key threats, and Fatal Push for early demons.
Side in Vanishing Verse, Duress, Go Blank, and Unlicensed Hearse. Target their Phoenix or key spells with interaction.
Side in Go Blank and Damping Sphere. Use Thoughtseize to find their answers and protect your combo pieces.
Side in Vanishing Verse. Use Fatal Push early and Sheltered by Ghosts to protect your creatures from their removal.
Side in Vanishing Verse, Go Blank, and Damping Sphere. Disrupt their spell sequencing and protect your graveyard.
Side in Vanishing Verse and Damping Sphere. Use interaction to slow their board development and protect your combo.
Side in Unlicensed Hearse. Protect your creatures with Sheltered by Ghosts and use discard outlets to trigger Monument before they sacrifice them.
Side in Go Blank and Unlicensed Hearse. Disrupt their Monument triggers and protect your own graveyard from their interaction.

common_threats: Aggressive creature decks (Mono Red Aggro, Angels, Spirits)

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Orzhov Greasefang is the latest variant... combining the already established plan of reanimating Parhelion II with Monument to Endurance and several creatures with free discard effects to trigger the artifact every turn, creating a synergistic plan while being difficult to counter with targeted answers.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: With Parhelion II in the graveyard, we can use Greasefang, Okiba Boss to return it to the battlefield with Haste and use Greasefang to crew the vehicle, attacking with it and creating two 4/4 Angel tokens for 13 damage.

Since we need to discard Parhelion II , we use Monument to Endurance with cards that allow repeated discard effects to generate value with the artifact, transforming it into a Ramp, a clock, and a source of card advantage.
Overlord of the Balemurk puts cards from the deck directly into the graveyard and allows you to reuse any creature each combat. It also functions as a less reliable win condition.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Since we need to discard Parhelion II , we use Monument to Endurance with cards that allow repeated discard effects to generate value with the artifact...
The Mycosynth Gardens function as extra copies of Monument to Endurance to stack more artifact triggers for each card we discard...
Since eight of our creatures can gain indestructible by discarding cards, we have relatively safe targets for the card [Sheltered by Ghosts] even in games with tons of removal.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: graveyard hates like Leyline of the Void and Rest in Peace
decks with small creatures like Mono Red Aggro, Angels, or Spirits
